title: carex subcernua
intro: Carex subcernua, also known as the sub-crenate sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Carex genus, typically found in wetland areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carex subcernua include its ability to help alleviate congestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Carex subcernua can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the dried leaves and roots in hot water, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
